name: Gabby Giffords
subject: I am amazed by yesterday's marches, Derek
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, www
body:
Yesterday we gave the gun lobby something to be worried about.
---
Derek, I wanted to make sure you saw my note yesterday.

I am amazed by the energy I saw at the March for Our Lives events. There is no doubt in my mind that we can defeat the gun lobby this year -- but only if we carry that same energy to the polls in November.

**Split a $3 donation between Giffords PAC and the DNC if you're ready to elect candidates who have the courage to reject the gun lobby and strengthen our gun laws.**

**[ DONATE: $3](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522549/4067b0a/527dc88a5/7e4b961d/1868804637/VEsEAA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/giffords-defeat-the-gun-lobby?amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=25+-+DONATE+3&utm_campaign=em_20180325_gg_nd&source=em_20180325_gg_nd&refcode=em_20180325_gg_nd")

[DONATE: $100](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522549/4067b0a/527dc88a5/7e4b9619/1868804637/VEsEDA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/giffords-defeat-the-gun-lobby?amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=29+-+DONATE+100&utm_campaign=em_20180325_gg_nd&source=em_20180325_gg_nd&refcode=em_20180325_gg_nd")
[Or donate another amount.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522549/4067b0a/527dc88a5/7e4b9626/1868804637/VEsFBQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/giffords-defeat-the-gun-lobby?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=30+-+Or+donate+another+amount&utm_campaign=em_20180325_gg_nd&source=em_20180325_gg_nd&refcode=em_20180325_gg_nd")
** Thank you for being a part of our movement to end gun violence.

Gabby Giffords

\---------- Forwarded message ----------
Sender: Gabby Giffords
Sent: Sat, Mar 24, 2018
Subject: I need your help to take on the gun lobby

Today we are giving the gun lobby something to be worried about.

At more than 800 events, our movement is taking a powerful stand against gun violence. We're showing that we will not accept horrific acts of violence as routine -- and if Congress won't act, then American voters will.

We can end the cycle of gun violence -- but it will only happen if we continue organizing to take on the gun lobby. So I am asking you today:

**Split a $3 donation between Giffords PAC and the DNC to defeat the gun lobby in November. It's time to elect candidates who will make our communities safer from gun violence.**

**[ DONATE: $3](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522549/4067b0a/527dc88a5/7e4b963f/1868804637/VEsDAA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/giffords-defeat-the-gun-lobby?amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=55+-+DONATE+3&utm_campaign=em_20180325_gg_nd&source=em_20180325_gg_nd&refcode=em_20180325_gg_nd")
[DONATE: $10](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522549/4067b0a/527dc88a5/7e4b963c/1868804637/VEsDAw/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/giffords-defeat-the-gun-lobby?amount=10&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=56+-+DONATE+10&utm_campaign=em_20180325_gg_nd&source=em_20180325_gg_nd&refcode=em_20180325_gg_nd")
[DONATE: $25](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522549/4067b0a/527dc88a5/7e4b963d/1868804637/VEsDAg/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/giffords-defeat-the-gun-lobby?amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=57+-+DONATE+25&utm_campaign=em_20180325_gg_nd&source=em_20180325_gg_nd&refcode=em_20180325_gg_nd")
[DONATE: $50](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522549/4067b0a/527dc88a5/7e4b963a/1868804637/VEsDDQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/giffords-defeat-the-gun-lobby?amount=50&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=58+-+DONATE+50&utm_campaign=em_20180325_gg_nd&source=em_20180325_gg_nd&refcode=em_20180325_gg_nd")
[DONATE: $100](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522549/4067b0a/527dc88a5/7e4b963b/1868804637/VEsDDA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/giffords-defeat-the-gun-lobby?amount=100&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=59+-+DONATE+100&utm_campaign=em_20180325_gg_nd&source=em_20180325_gg_nd&refcode=em_20180325_gg_nd")
[Or donate another amount.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522549/4067b0a/527dc88a5/7e4b9638/1868804637/VEsABQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/giffords-defeat-the-gun-lobby?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=60+-+Or+donate+another+amount&utm_campaign=em_20180325_gg_nd&source=em_20180325_gg_nd&refcode=em_20180325_gg_nd")
** The gun lobby is fully committed to defending their deadly agenda, no matter what the cost. They are prepared to spend tens of millions of dollars this year -- all to elect candidates who will stall efforts on gun violence prevention legislation.

This year we must match their commitment with our own. We must remember that we are not hopeless in the fight to end gun violence -- and our collective power at marches nationwide today proves that.

**If our current leaders do not have the courage to act on gun violence, then it's up to us to elect candidates who will.**

I am going to work tirelessly this year to defeat politicians who are funded by the gun lobby -- and today I am counting on you to join me.

**[Split a $3 donation between Giffords PAC and the DNC today. Your contribution will help end gun violence and save lives.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522549/4067b0a/527dc88a5/7e4b9639/1868804637/VEsABA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/giffords-defeat-the-gun-lobby?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=61+-+Split+a+if+consinConsGroup29378+nother+e&utm_campaign=em_20180325_gg_nd&source=em_20180325_gg_nd&refcode=em_20180325_gg_nd")**

All my best,

Gabby Giffords

subject: BREAKING: Trump moves to ban most transgender troops
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
Derek,

[Late Friday evening, President Trump took another step in making his hateful pledge from last year to ban transgender people from the military a reality.

While he announced this mean-spirited and unnecessary decision on Twitter last year, it had not yet been enacted as policy. This newest statement from President Trump marks a tangible decision he could enforce immediately.

](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=DiNnXueqokE44BrJ11qo2frZppvWO550)**[We can't let this administration roll back the progress we've made towards LGBTQ equality, and neither will Tim Kaine. Will you add your name to stand with us? >>](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=afNTw8yzfHQ6E2EFOih%2FEvrZppvWO550)**

Tim knows that transgender Americans choosing to serve are heroes - and that anyone who is qualified to serve deserves to be able to. Threatening their health care and shaming them for their medical needs is nothing short of disgraceful. That's why we're standing up to call this latest attack on the LGBTQ community what it is - an extreme right-wing push to take away the progress we have made.

**[If you agree with Tim that discrimination in any form is unnacceptable, sign the petition today >>](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=tLVigr2ce7ZWvbQ8HXe8nPrZppvWO550)**

Thank you,

_Team Kaine_

name: Team Kasich
subject: The latest
body:
Thank you for your continued interest and support. Please find a
quick recap of recent news and activities in this email.

Peter, thank you for your
continued interest and support. Below is a recap of recent
activities and the latest news:

The Ohio Model Works:

The latest jobs report showed
Ohio had one of its best job creating months since the onset of
the Kasich Administration. More than 19,000 jobs were created and
Ohio doubled the national rate of job growth in February.
Additionally, the unemployment rate improved to 4.5% - the lowest
it's been since 2001.

All told, Ohioans have created
493,500 private sector jobs since John Kasich became governor.

Balanced budgets - lower taxes -
reducing burdensome red tape & regulations - investments in the
jobs of the future - this is Ohio's model under Gov. Kasich's
leadership and it's a model for other states and the nation.

CNN - March 25, 2018:

Gov. Kasich spoke about the
absence of American leadership in the world and voiced his
support for those young Americans who are standing up and calling
for action from our government:

If you agree that we can put in
place common sense gun reforms that will save lives, please take
a moment to sign our petition and help us get this message out
far and wide: JohnKasich.com/CommonSenseGunLaws

Enough is Enough:

$1.3 Trillion - That's how large
the spending bill is that Congress just passed and the President
signed.

This will add hundreds of
billions to our runaway national debt and it only funds the
federal government until September!

On Thursday, our national debt
officially crossed $21 TRILLION and counting - and we're on pace
to reach $22 trillion in a year. We hit $20 trillion just last
September and $19 trillion in 2016!

That is a staggering
mismanagement of our government and future generations of
Americans will pay dearly for the negligence of our elected
leaders!

A New Way for California
Republicans:

Gov. Kasich joined former
California Governor Arnold Schwarzenegger at an event supporting
New Way California, an organization fighting to return civility
to our national discussion and push Republicans in California to
champion common-sense reforms that ignore the politics and just
do what's right for the people.

Don't Miss This one!

Here is a clip of Gov.
Schwarzenegger speaking with Michael Smerconish about his support
for Gov. Kasich's message:

Thank you for all that you're
doing to help!

-Team Kasich

P.S. In you'd like to chip in to
help us continue paying for the tools to advance Gov. Kasich's
critical message, please consider making a donation (here).

Paid for by Kasich
for America, not authorized by any candidate or candidate's
committee.
www.JohnKasich.com

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: Gun violence is an intersectional problem.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor
body:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3128/9e00690/45abf6d0/240244b9/970016891/VEsH/ "https://andrewgillum.com/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em18_180325_March_engg_gq_act__AG&source=em18_180325_March_engg_gq_act__AG")
Friend,

Yesterday millions of people across the world marched to demand an end to gun violence. They were moved to action by Parkland, Charleston, Sandy Hook, Pulse Nightclub, and too many other places to name.

But they were also moved by the senseless gun violence that affects cities and communities of color every single day, that doesn't make the national news. We can call some of their names and faces, like Stephon Clark and Hadiya Pendleton, but not nearly enough of them.

Naomi Wadler, an 11-year-old activist, reminded us of this reality during her speech at the March for Our Lives in Washington, DC:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3128/9e00690/45abf6d0/240244b8/970016891/VEsE/ "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mfuaMRYPIVI&feature=youtu.be&utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=2&utm_campaign=em18_180325_March_engg_gq_act__AG&source=em18_180325_March_engg_gq_act__AG")
Naomi, like so many of us, understands that gun violence is an intersectional problem. It doesn't just affect the students of a Broward County high school. It affects black and brown youth and their families in every corner of our country. It affects students in rural communities nationwide.

Their lives must also mean something to our public policy makers, and yesterday people of every ethnicity, gender, religion, orientation, identity, and ability showed the world that ending gun violence in every community is an urgent call for us now.

The March for Our Lives showed that there are more of us than there are of them. We're going to win this fight, because the power of our united voices and votes is stronger than the gun lobby's status quo.

**[Add your name now and join me in standing in solidarity with the all the students and marchers who participated yesterday -- and the fight forward. Together, we can keep the pressure on and make #NeverAgain a reality.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3128/9e00690/45abf6d0/240244bb/970016891/VEsF/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/add-your-name-support-our-students?utm_campaign=em18_180325_March_engg_gq_act__AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&source=em18_180325_March_engg_gq_act__AG&utm_content=3+-+Add+your+name+now+and+join+me+in+standin")**

Bring it home,

Andrew

Paid for by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor.

Andrew Gillum for Governor
1550 Melvin Street,
Tallahassee, FL 32303

| | | March 25, 2018
---
| Leah's been busy traveling the state, and we've got a lot of news to share!
---
| First up, Leah's son Nicholas, who is a First Lieutenant in the U.S. Army graduated from Ranger School. A very proud mom, Leah was able to travel down to Fort Benning to pin the Ranger tab on his uniform.
---
|
---
---
| Some excellent news → At caucuses across Wisconsin, Leah's been winning more and more support from the grassroots. This weekend after forums with her primary opponent, she won both the 2nd and 4th Congressional District Republican caucuses with 71% and 80%, respectively. Congrats, Leah!
---
|
---
---
|
---
---
| More good news: Wisconsin has hit **2.9% unemployment** — an all-time low!

As a result of reforms Leah has passed with Gov. Walker, a balanced budget, $8 billion in tax cuts and the elimination of job-killing regulations, Wisconsin is undeniably the greatest state in the nation to live, work and raise a family.
---
| Leah started a Sweet 16 bracket of her victories for Wisconsin — which are why she's the consistent conservative we can count on to go to Washington to fight against illegal immigration, stand up for our pro-life values and defend our constitutional rights.

Check out which issues made it to the Elite 8.
---
|
---
---
|

Walker, a balanced budget, $8 billion in tax cuts and the elimination of job-killing regulations, Wisconsin is undeniably the greatest state in the nation to live, work and raise a family. | Leah started a Sweet 16 bracket of her victories for Wisconsin — which are why she's the consistent conservative we can count on to go to Washington to fight against illegal immigration, stand up for our pro-life values and defend our constitutional rights. Check out which issues made it to the Elite 8. | | | Vote in Round 2 of the tournament here → | First Lady Tonette Walker has announced her support for Leah's campaign. | | "Fearlessly, Leah pushed forward helping to lower property taxes, expand opportunities for education and ensure our state is open for business. But now she’s on another mission — to take the Wisconsin way of hard work, bold ideas and never giving up to Washington." Read her op-ed . | This week, Leah and her daughter, Elena, were invited by President Trump to join him for a celebration of Greek Independence Day. | | | Leah's continuing to gain momentum. She's announced that she has — including business leaders, law enforcement, elected officials, grassroots activists and college students — backing her campaign. | | She also gained the support of Winning For Women, a national group devoted to supporting women who champion free markets and a strong military. Read about the endorsement . | As always, follow Leah's travels on her Wisconsin Way tour by following her , and pages. On Wisconsin! Team Leah | News from the campaign | In a pair of tweets, Leah Vukmir ripped into U.S. Rep. Mark Pocan Monday for offering a job to fired FBI deputy director Andrew McCabe and proposed making it illegal to hire a federal employee to pad a pension. | "When a Republican woman stands for our Wisconsin principles, votes the right way and is ready to shake things up for America, it is our duty to support her. We can’t just say we want more representatives who get results. We have to actively support the ladies who fight to make a real difference. That’s why I choose Leah. "It’s time to take Leah’s heart, conservative ideals, tireless work ethic and proven success to Washington. Please join me in supporting her as she takes the courageous step toward serving Wisconsin as our next U.S. senator." | A GOP group that backs female candidates who support free-market policies and national security unveiled its second round of endorsements in top Senate and House races. In an announcement that was shared first with The Hill, Winning For Women is backing six candidates — one in the Senate and five in the House — in some of the most competitive races of 2018. In the Senate, the organization is backing state Sen. Leah Vukmir, who’s running for the GOP nomination to take on Sen. Tammy Baldwin (D-Wis.). | Leah Vukmir, a Wisconsin Republican running for Senate, wants a fair hearing for Gordon Giampietro, nominated by President Trump to fill a federal district court seat. | Whereas Nicholson is relying on support from inside the Beltway, Vukmir’s hard work with fighting for conservative principles has paid off in the form of a mountain support from state legislators, activists and many young conservatives on college campuses. She now has over 400 endorsements. A Students Choose Leah coalition has been formed, with a total of 140 college students. Some of the most prominent endorsements include First Lady Tonette Walker and former White House Chief of Staff Reince Priebus. This level support gives the Vukmir camp the opportunity to mobilize legions of conservatives as the primary season continues. | Leah Vukmir, a Republican state senator from Brookfield, Wisconsin running for Tammy Baldwin's Senate seat, is calling out a Wisconsin Supreme Court candidate for the company she is keeping on her campaign. Rebecca Dallet accepted former Attorney General Eric Holder's support in her campaign for the state supreme court. Vukmir said it is unacceptable, considering Holder's refusal to cooperate with Congress during an investigation into the Operation Fast and Furious scandal. | "In my experience as an educator, I know no one is better to advocate for their children than a mom on a mission. That’s exactly why I’m supporting Leah Vukmir to be our next U.S. Senator from Wisconsin." | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Paid for by Leah for Senate |

name: Mark Kelly
subject: The gun lobby is nervous, Derek
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, www
body:
If there's one thing Gabby and I have learned, it's that the gun lobby never goes down without a fight.
---
When Gabby and I started our campaign to end gun violence, we never imagined a day like yesterday, Derek.

Marching alongside tens of thousands of kids demanding their leaders take action to keep them safe from guns in their schools and communities was so inspiring -- these students showed more courage and conviction than the Republican-led Congress ever has. Young people are taking gun violence seriously, and they expect their elected leaders to do the same.

But if there's one thing Gabby and I have learned since committing ourselves to the campaign to change our country's gun laws, it's that the gun lobby never backs down -- and you can bet they will double down on their efforts after seeing our movement rallying together at the March for Our Lives events across the country.

The gun lobby is nervous, Derek -- that's for sure. But it's up to us to keep it that way.

**Split a $3 donation between Giffords PAC and the DNC. Your donation will help defeat the gun lobby in all 50 states this November.**

This November is our chance to end the gun lobby's influence on Congress and state legislatures -- but it will only happen if we elect our own coalition of leaders in Congress who are ready to take action on gun violence.

And it's that we can't afford to miss our mark.

Representing Ohio in the Senate is one of the most important things I'll ever do. I don't take it lightly. Every day I go into work with the same goal: to fight for working families. Often times that means standing up to lobbyists, big banks, insurance companies -- and the lawmakers they support.

But I'll never stop fighting this fight. My work is not over until we've fixed our system so people working 40 hours a week can afford health coverage, put food on the table for their families, save for retirement, and be able to enjoy their lives -- maybe even go to the ballpark now and then.

**This is exactly why special interest groups are against me.** They would much rather elect a senator who will be a shill for corporate interests than a champion for Ohio families.

We can strike out special interest groups. We overcame their spending in 2012 -- and we will again as long as our grassroots team shows up to play from now until November. Help us get on track to reach our FEC goal.

Sherrod

name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: Playing Jenga with health care
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
body:
|
---
| | We're celebrating two important health care anniversaries this month, team. The first is the eighth anniversary of the Affordable Care Act. Eight years ago, President Obama signed landmark legislation that pushed insurers to cover essential benefits and prohibited them from denying Americans health care because of pre-existing conditions. [_**Sign here to protect the ACA. We 're trying to get to 8,888 signatures. Add your name to mine.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113fe7/622b7b5b/19e96349/307b8d10/1823007050/VEsH/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180323") This brings me to the other anniversary. One year ago, we defeated Trumpcare -- which would have repealed the Affordable Care Act and gutted protections for people with pre-existing conditions. Unfortunately, Republicans haven't stopped playing Jenga with our health care system. President Trump stopped payments to cost-sharing reimbursements and the Republican tax law repealed the individual mandate. It's clear Republicans and President Trump are trying to pull out enough pieces of the ACA so it tumbles. [_**We need to fight back and continue to protect it. Add your signature to stand up for the ACA.**_](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113fe7/622b7b5b/19e96349/307b8d10/1823007050/VEsE/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/e180323") Thank you for taking action, Debbie
